pattern: Indexing the running best length by (end index, common difference) lets each pair extend a prior chain in O(1), turning an exponential search into a quadratic DP.
hints: An arithmetic subsequence is fully described by where it ends and its common difference.
For each pair i < j, the run ending at j with difference nums[j] - nums[i] is one longer than the best run ending at i with that same difference.
Keep a table keyed by (index, difference); fill it for all pairs and the answer is the largest stored length.

edge_inputs.description: decreasing arithmetic run hidden among others
all identical gives a long zero-difference run

stress.description: random values in range with frequent repeats to stress the difference table
stress.note: size capped so the O(n^2) reference stays fast
